prompt:
While researching a historic theater restoration, a student took the following notes:
• The theater opened in 1912.
• A fire damaged it badly in 1980.
• Donors funded a full restoration this year.

The student wants to present the building's troubled history. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Opened in 1912, the theater was nearly destroyed by a fire in 1980 before donors finally funded a full restoration this year.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to present the building's troubled history.

choices.text: A theater, a building made for staging plays and concerts, often serves as a gathering place for a whole community.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not present the building's troubled history as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Thanks to a group of generous donors, the theater received a complete restoration during the course of this past year.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not present the building's troubled history as the prompt asks.

choices.text: When the theater first opened its doors to audiences back in 1912, it quickly became a centerpiece of the town.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not present the building's troubled history as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to present the building's troubled history; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a desert solar tower, a student took the following notes:
• Mirrors focus sunlight on a central tower.
• Molten salt stores the heat for nightfall.
• The plant supplies power after dark.

The student wants to explain how the plant produces power at night. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: By storing the sun's heat in molten salt, the tower keeps supplying power well after dark, when ordinary solar panels go quiet.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to explain how the plant produces power at night.

choices.text: A wide field of mirrors surrounds the plant, each one angled to focus sunlight onto the tall central tower.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the plant produces power at night as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The tower at the heart of the plant is the point where all of the mirrors direct their concentrated sunlight.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the plant produces power at night as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Solar power, drawn from the energy of the sun, can be captured in several different ways across the world.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the plant produces power at night as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to explain how the plant produces power at night; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:

choices.text: A bee, an insect that gathers nectar and pollen, lives within a hive alongside thousands of its fellow workers.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the benefit to surrounding plants as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Because the surrounding city offers blooms for much of the year, the bees enjoy an unusually long season.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the benefit to surrounding plants as the prompt asks.

choices.text: From their hives atop the downtown office, the bees range out to pollinate the flowering gardens of nearby parks.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to highlight the benefit to surrounding plants.

choices.text: The beehives that anchor the project are set up on the flat rooftop of a tall downtown office building.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the benefit to surrounding plants as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to highlight the benefit to surrounding plants; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:

choices.text: After the town added curbside glass pickup, glass recycling jumped 60% and landfill waste dropped noticeably.

choices.text: Recycling, the process of collecting used materials so they can be remade, is practiced by many towns in different ways.

choices.text: Glass recycling in the town rose by 60% over a single year, a figure that the program's organizers found encouraging.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the program's impact as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The town decided to add curbside glass pickup so that residents would no longer need to haul their bottles to a center.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the program's impact as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to emphasize the program's impact; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a public seed library, a student took the following notes:
• Gardeners borrow seeds for free.
• They return seeds saved from their harvest.
• The collection favors local heirloom varieties.

The student wants to explain how the library sustains itself. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Any gardener in the community is welcome to borrow a packet of seeds from the library at no cost at all.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the library sustains itself as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The seed library keeps going because gardeners who borrow seeds for free return new ones saved from their own harvests.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to explain how the library sustains itself.

choices.text: A seed, the small package from which a plant grows, can stay viable for years if it is stored properly.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the library sustains itself as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The collection housed at the seed library leans heavily toward heirloom varieties suited to the local climate.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain how the library sustains itself as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to explain how the library sustains itself; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a coral spawning event, a student took the following notes:
• The corals spawn one night each year.
• They release eggs in sync with the full moon.
• Divers collect the eggs to raise new colonies.

The student wants to convey the remarkable timing of the spawning. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Cued by the full moon, an entire reef of corals releases its eggs on a single synchronized night each year.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to convey the remarkable timing of the spawning.

choices.text: On the night of the spawning, divers carefully gather the floating eggs in order to raise brand-new colonies.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey the remarkable timing of the spawning as the prompt asks.

choices.text: The eggs that the corals release during the event are collected and later used to grow fresh coral colonies.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey the remarkable timing of the spawning as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Coral, a tiny animal that builds a stony skeleton, joins with countless others to form a sprawling living reef.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not convey the remarkable timing of the spawning as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to convey the remarkable timing of the spawning; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:

• Fragments are replanted on damaged reefs.

The student wants to explain the method and its success. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Working below the surface, divers carefully grow small coral fragments on specially built underwater frames.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain the method and its success as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Coral reefs, which shelter a quarter of all marine species, are among the most important ecosystems in the ocean.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain the method and its success as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Divers grow coral fragments on underwater frames and replant them on damaged reefs, where more than 70% survive.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to explain the method and its success.

choices.text: More than 70% of the transplanted coral fragments survive, a rate the nursery's divers consider a genuine success.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain the method and its success as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to explain the method and its success; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:

choices.text: Cycling, an inexpensive and healthy way to get around, has grown more popular in many crowded urban centers.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not explain why people took up cycling as the prompt asks.

choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to explain why people took up cycling.

prompt:

choices.text: The gain in the number of species is most likely the result of the marshland that was restored in recent years.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the increase over time as the prompt asks.

choices.text: This spring, the volunteer counters carefully logged a total of 84 different bird species across the wetland.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not highlight the increase over time as the prompt asks.

choices.text: Spring counts have climbed from 61 species a decade ago to 84 today, a gain likely driven by restored marshland.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to highlight the increase over time.

explanation: The goal is to highlight the increase over time; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.

prompt:
While researching a hummingbird study, a student took the following notes:
• Hummingbirds beat their wings up to 80 times per second.
• They have the fastest metabolism of any bird.
• A researcher recorded their heart rates in the wild.

The student wants to emphasize the birds' extreme physiology. Which choice most effectively uses the relevant information to accomplish this goal?

choices.text: Although hummingbirds beat their wings quickly, the study focused mainly on how a researcher recorded their heart rates outdoors.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the birds' extreme physiology as the prompt asks.

choices.text: With wings beating up to 80 times per second and the fastest metabolism of any bird, hummingbirds push avian physiology to its limits.
choices.rationale: Correct. This sentence uses the notes to emphasize the birds' extreme physiology.

choices.text: Hummingbirds, which a researcher studied in the wild, are among the many bird species that beat their wings to stay aloft.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the birds' extreme physiology as the prompt asks.

choices.text: A researcher who recorded hummingbirds' heart rates in the wild found that they have the fastest metabolism of any bird.
choices.rationale: This is accurate but does not emphasize the birds' extreme physiology as the prompt asks.

explanation: The goal is to emphasize the birds' extreme physiology; only the correct choice selects and combines the notes to do that.
